scope: Tavo native plugin, media, theme, provider-form, package, and backup behavior only

F01.claim: A root entry-only plugin can execute without UI contributions.

F03.claim: When entry and scripts.actions coexist, only entry dispatches.

F04.claim: plugin.config.get/all returns effective defaults and overrides; mutating all() output does not persist.

passed: one persistent assistant add after streaming
F05.regression: A controlled chat update did not deliver chat:updated to a chat:changed compatibility handler.

F06.claim: input hooks observed UI, TavoJS, and MCP sources; rewrite, cancel, handler-local fail-open, timeout, and afterSend acceptance timing worked.
F06.boundary: Attachment preservation was not exposed and is not claimed.

F07.claim: generation:prepare rewrote only the transient final-user model request, kept the saved user message original, and preserved steady multi-plugin order.

F08.claim: generation:success changed text before save; empty, thrown, and timed-out rewrites failed open to the original reply.

passed: partial and empty cancellation persistence split
F09.regression: The controlled fourth-source path emitted neither an othersContinuation hook event nor a persisted message.
F09.blocked: Auxiliary independent, image, speech, and summary exclusion was not proved as one controlled set.

F10.claim: tavo.input.send returned the documented success/failure shape and resolved at acceptance before generation completion.
F10.boundary: The busy reason was not observed.

blocked: Neither explicit single-target call was accepted in the original unconfigured voice environment.
Stop completed, but the original matrix had no accepted queued item and therefore could not prove queue clearing.
F11.manual: A working provider plus human listening was required before single-target playback, shared-stop behavior, and audible identity could be claimed. Later deterministic-gateway reconciliation is recorded only under supplemental.tts.

claims: bold and italic font style read back
role and user bubble sides read back
visual-novel 30 percent and 70 percent previews differed
role-only and user-only rule scopes saved and reopened
temporary rule was deleted and switches restored

asr.integration: A deterministic OpenAI-compatible gateway received multipart WAV transcription requests; release-to-send, explicit cancel, fill-input, edit, clear, and bounded transport-failure recovery were observed.
asr.manual: Human-spoken recognition accuracy was not evaluated.
asr.boundary: ASR remains absent from the current official crawl and MCP surface.

tts.integration: A deterministic OpenAI-compatible gateway received /v1/audio/speech JSON requests and returned WAV; configured character playback and queue calls were accepted.
tts.reconciliation: This supplemental run narrows the original F11 result without overwriting its immutable unconfigured-environment findings.
tts.blocked: Persona targeting was not retested with a working binding.
tts.manual: Audible identity, quality, and stop semantics were not evaluated by a human listener.

image.integration: A deterministic OpenAI-compatible gateway received /v1/images/generations and the one-pixel fixture produced a chat image message.
image.boundary: This does not prove visual quality or real-provider compatibility.

novelAi.claim: A disposable configuration could be force-saved after a fake-key network failure, reopened, and deleted.
novelAi.boundary: No real NovelAI request or credential was used.

nonSubstitutionRules: Do not promote F05, F09, or F11 as blanket passes.
A deterministic gateway proves integration shape, not human media quality or every real provider.
UI save and reopen does not prove provider wire compatibility.
A package dry-run does not prove installation, persistence, update preservation, or backup restoration.
